name: Physics Weight Puzzle
description: Players use object mass, placement, buoyancy, gravity, or balance to satisfy a physical puzzle condition.
design_notes: Weight puzzles must define which object_instance_id values count, how mass is measured, what reset policy prevents lost objects, and how physics variance is bounded. Solvability checks should verify that required objects can still reach target areas after player mistakes.
design_purpose: Turn physical object manipulation into readable puzzle logic.
Connect gravity, buoyancy, pressure plates, moving platforms, and object placement.
Let players solve with spatial reasoning while still feeling grounded in world physics.
player_fantasy: The player feels they are using real weight and placement to make the environment respond.

parameters.name: weight_puzzle_id
parameters.description: Stable puzzle identifier used by validation, saves, and authoring tools.

parameters.name: object_instance_id
parameters.description: Tracked object that can contribute mass or position to the puzzle.

parameters.name: mass_threshold_units
parameters.description: Required accepted mass before the puzzle condition is satisfied.

parameters.name: target_zone_id
parameters.description: Zone where tracked objects must rest or balance to count.

parameters.name: reset_policy_id
parameters.description: Rule that restores objects after falling, despawning, or soft-locking the puzzle.

parameters.name: solvability_check_id
parameters.description: Authoring or runtime check that confirms the puzzle can still be solved.

edge_cases: A required object falls into an unreachable pit or is destroyed by another system.
Physics sleep makes an object appear stable while tiny motion still changes the solved state.
Two lighter objects stack in a way the designer did not intend but still pass the mass threshold.
A saved game restores the object but not its velocity, sleep state, or target-zone membership.
common_bugs: Every collider on one object contributes mass separately and over-activates the puzzle.
Object reset runs while the player is carrying the object and teleports it away.
Solvability tools ignore locked doors or one-way ledges that block object return routes.
Network clients disagree on mass totals because physics authority is not centralized.
balancing_notes: Object classes should visually communicate relative weight so solutions feel deduced, not guessed.
Physics tolerance is important; near-solved arrangements should not flicker rapidly.
Manual reset should be nearby and fast when object manipulation is slow.
accessibility_notes: Provide readable target zones and optional hints for which object classes can contribute.
Avoid requiring precise analog object placement without snap, guide, or reset support.

implementation_notes.unity: Track unique Rigidbody roots by object_instance_id inside target zones and compute accepted mass from data, not collider count. Persist object transform, velocity, sleep state, and reset anchor so save/load does not change puzzle outcome.
implementation_notes.godot: Use Area2D or Area3D zones plus tracked body ids, and reconcile zone membership during physics ticks. Reset should restore object_instance_id to reset anchors only after checking player carry or interaction state.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use server or single-player authority to evaluate mass thresholds from component data and overlap sets. Debug draw target zones and accepted objects for level authors, and persist physics body state for required objects.
implementation_notes.web: For Matter.js, Phaser, or custom physics, keep puzzle logic in a deterministic object tracker rather than reading visual positions. Add lost-object checks that move required objects back to reset anchors safely.

example_games.title: Portal
example_games.note: Uses weighted cubes and buttons as repeated spatial puzzle elements.

example_games.title: Half-Life 2
example_games.note: Uses physical object weight and placement in environmental puzzles.
